questions.text: What is the primary philosophy behind 'vibe coding'?

options.text: Using LLMs to write the majority of code while the developer orchestrates

questions.text: Which of the following best describes the role of a developer in a vibe coding workflow?
What is a critical skill required for effective vibe coding?

options.text: Prompt engineering and providing clear context to LLMs

questions.text: Which of the following tools is most commonly associated with a modern vibe coding workflow?
In vibe coding, what is the best practice when an LLM generates code that doesn't work?

options.text: Delete the entire file and start over manually
Provide the error message and context back to the LLM to guide it towards the fix
Ignore the error and deploy to production
Switch to a different programming language

questions.text: How does vibe coding change the traditional software development lifecycle?

options.text: It eliminates the need for testing entirely
It shifts focus from manual syntax typing to high-level system design and logic verification
It prevents developers from ever looking at the source code
It requires all code to be written on mobile devices

questions.text: What does MCP stand for in the context of modern agentic workflows?
Why is 'context' considered the most important asset in vibe coding?

options.text: Because LLMs need relevant files and instructions to generate accurate, project-specific code
Because context determines the color theme of the IDE
Because it speeds up the hardware compilation time
Because it reduces the internet bandwidth used by the LLM

questions.text: What is a major pitfall to avoid when vibe coding?

options.text: Blindly accepting generated code without reviewing or understanding the logic
